growth_records.strain: C5 (isolated from nitrate-contaminated groundwater, Oak Ridge National Laboratory, TN)
growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: grown with 8 mM lactate as carbon source and electron donor and 3 ml of sterile air injected ... we were able to calculate a specific growth rate of 0.4 ± 0.02 h−1 and a generation time of 1.73 ± 0.09 h (Figure 4B Inset)
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch on lactate+O2 minimal medium, 30C, 225 rpm, pH 7.2 (bicarbonate-buffered). Reported mu=0.4 +/- 0.02 h-1; doubling_time 1.73 +/- 0.09 h (reported); consistent with ln2/0.4=1.733 h. Medium minimal salts listed; nitrate not present in this O2-fed condition. No uptake/secretion rates measured. Full lactate/nitrate base medium recipe given in Methods (trace elements/vitamins per Yoon 2013 and Hillesland 2014, not itemized here).

growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: anaerobic versus microaerobic conditions, which were also found to have slightly different growth rates (Figure 5A; microaerobic μ = 0.2 h−1; anaerobic μ = 0.18 h−1)
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch, microaerobic lactate/nitrate minimal medium, 30C, pH 7.2, 225 rpm. Reported mu=0.2 h-1. doubling_time_h=3.47 CALCULATED = ln2/0.2 = 0.6931/0.2 = 3.466 h (not reported). No uptake/secretion rates. Lactate concentration not restated for this condition (8 mM used in the O2 condition); nitrate present as alternative electron acceptor.

growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: microaerobic μ = 0.2 h−1; anaerobic μ = 0.18 h−1
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch, anaerobic lactate/nitrate minimal medium, 30C, pH 7.2, 225 rpm. Reported mu=0.18 h-1. doubling_time_h=3.85 CALCULATED = ln2/0.18 = 0.6931/0.18 = 3.851 h (not reported). Nitrate-reducing (denitrification + respiratory ammonification). No uptake/secretion rates measured.

growth_records.cite: Vuono, David C. et al. A Real-Time Multiplexed Microbial Growth Intervalometer for Capturing High-Resolution Growth Curves. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01135. [PMC6560151]
growth_records.snippet: The cultures growing in complex media (LB) under microaerobic conditions exhibited highly reproducible growth rates (μ = 0.24 ± 0.004 h−1)
growth_records.notes: I. calvum C5 batch on complex LB medium, microaerobic, 30C, 225 rpm. Reported mu=0.24 +/- 0.004 h-1. doubling_time_h=2.89 CALCULATED = ln2/0.24 = 0.6931/0.24 = 2.888 h (not reported). LB composition not itemized (named only). No uptake/secretion rates measured.

growth_records.medium: Aerobic minimal medium; 8 mM lactate + O2 + 1.5 mM ammonium as N-source (no nitrate)

up.method: consumed (ion chromatography) but no specific per-biomass rate reported

growth_records.cite: Vuono DC et al. Resource Concentration Modulates the Fate of Dissimilated Nitrogen in a Dual-Pathway Actinobacterium. Front Microbiol (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00003. [PMC6349771]
growth_records.snippet: when grown on 8 mM lactate, O2, and 1.5 mM ammonium as N-source, I. calvum displayed a typical growth curve with a specific growth rate of 0.4 +/- 0.02 mu (1.7 +/- 0.1 doublings/hour)
growth_records.notes: Aerobic reference growth. mu = 0.4 +/- 0.02 /h reported directly. td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.4 = 1.73 h. NOTE: paper's parenthetical '1.7 +/- 0.1 doublings/hour' is internally inconsistent with mu=0.4 (mu=0.4 corresponds to 0.4/ln2 = 0.58 doublings/h, not 1.7); treated as a paper reporting inconsistency, mu retained. Lactate/O2 not quantified as specific rates.

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic nitrate-reducing minimal medium; 8 mM lactate + 12 mM nitrate (C:NO3=2), ammonium-replete then ammonium-free state transition

up.method: consumed over time series (Fig 3A, IC); not fully consumed; no specific rate reported
fully reduced to nitrite (IC); growth occurred on nitrate reduction

sec.units: umol total per 48 h state-transition incubation
sec.method: ion chromatography; net NH4+ produced (ammonium-free state transition)

sec.units: umol total per 48 h state-transition incubation

sec.method: major extracellular end-product of nitrate respiration (IC)
100-200 uM range, minor incomplete lactate oxidation (IC); lactate predominantly oxidized fully to CO2

growth_records.cite: Vuono DC et al. Resource Concentration Modulates the Fate of Dissimilated Nitrogen in a Dual-Pathway Actinobacterium. Front Microbiol (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00003. [PMC6349771]
growth_records.snippet: Net ammonium production was 13 +/- 2.7 umoles with the remainder of dissimilated N being used by the denitrification pathway (24 +/- 4.2 umoles N2O-N), resulting in a recovery of 97.4% dissimilated N.
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic nitrate respiration, C:NO3=2. Growth occurred primarily on nitrate reduction (Fig 3A); nitrite reduction to NH4+/N2O in stationary phase. Cell counts increased 29% (factor 3.5x, 1.8 generations, net 5.4e5 cells/mL) over 48 h in ammonium-free media. Dissimilation products reported as TOTAL umoles per incubation, NOT specific per-biomass rates; per-treatment mu shown only graphically (Fig 2C) and specific rates require Supplementary Tables (not available in fulltext XML) -- cannot compute qS/q-product. Acetate 100-200 uM minor overflow.

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic minimal medium; 8 mM lactate + 12 mM nitrite (C:NO2=2), ammonium-replete

up.method: supplied as sole acceptor; slowly depleted (IC)

growth_records.cite: Vuono DC et al. Resource Concentration Modulates the Fate of Dissimilated Nitrogen in a Dual-Pathway Actinobacterium. Front Microbiol (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00003. [PMC6349771]
growth_records.snippet: When cultures were given nitrite, instead of nitrate as a terminal electron acceptor (8 mM lactate, 12 mM nitrite; ratio = 2), we observed no immediate growth ... 33.4 +/- 4.8 umoles N2O-N and 8.0 +/- 2.5 umoles NH4+
growth_records.notes: Nitrite as sole acceptor: no immediate growth; denitrification (N2O) exceeded ammonification. Products reported as TOTAL umoles per incubation (Supplementary Fig S5), not specific per-biomass rates; no biomass/time numeric values in text to compute rates. Included as experimental anaerobic nitrite-respiration secretion end-products.
